contexts: This Standard supersedes the following Standards and Interpretations: (a) IAS 17 Leases; (b) IFRIC 4 Determining whether an Arrangement contains a Lease; (c) SIC-15 Operating Leases – Incentives; and (d) SIC-27 Evaluating the Substance of Transactions Involving the Legal Form of Lease.
IFRS 16 Lease liability in a sale and leaseback applies retrospectively in accordance with IAS 8 to sale and leaseback transactions entered into after the date of initial application.
samples.ground_truth: IAS 17, IFRIC 4, SIC-15, and SIC-27

samples.question: When must an entity apply the amendments to other Standards as a consequence of IASB issuing IFRS 16?
contexts: An entity shall apply the amendments for annual periods beginning on or after 1 January 2019.
If an entity applies this Standard for an earlier period, it shall also apply these amendments for that earlier period.
samples.ground_truth: For annual periods beginning on or after 1 January 2019

samples.question: What date were certain Standards not effective?
contexts: The Standards were not effective at 1 January 2016.
For Standards that were not effective on 1 January 2016, the amendments in this appendix are presented based on the text of the initial publication of that Standard.
samples.ground_truth: 1 January 2016

samples.question: Who dissented from the approval of IFRS 16 Leases?
contexts: IFRS 16 Leases was approved for issue by thirteen of the fourteen members of the International Accounting Standards Board.
Mr Zhang dissented.
samples.ground_truth: Mr Zhang

samples.question: How many members of the International Accounting Standards Board approved the issue of Covid-19-Related Rent Concessions in May 2020?
contexts: Covid-19-Related Rent Concessions, which amended IFRS 16, was approved for issue by all 14 members of the International Accounting Standards Board.
The International Accounting Standards Board consists of members including Hans Hoogervorst, Suzanne Lloyd, and others.

samples.question: How many members of the International Accounting Standards Board approved the issue of Interest Rate Benchmark Reform—Phase 2?
contexts: Interest Rate Benchmark Reform—Phase 2 was approved for issue by 12 of 13 members of the International Accounting Standards Board (Board).
Mr Gast abstained in view of his recent appointment to the Board.

samples.question: How many members of the International Accounting Standards Board approved the amendment to IFRS 16 for Covid-19-related rent concessions beyond 30 June 2021?
contexts: Approval by the Board of Covid-19-Related Rent Concessions beyond 30 June 2021, which amended IFRS 16, was approved for issue by 11 of 13 members of the International Accounting Standards Board.
Messrs Anderson and Gast dissented.

samples.question: When was Lease Liability in a Sale and Leaseback approved by the IASB?
contexts: Lease Liability in a Sale and Leaseback, which amends IFRS 16 Leases, was approved for issue by nine of ten members of the International Accounting Standards Board (IASB) in September 2022
samples.ground_truth: September 2022

samples.question: What is the primary objective of project scope definition in project management?
contexts: The objective of project scope definition is to ensure the project aligns with the organization's strategic goals and is feasible to deliver.
Project scope definition involves conducting market research, analyzing organizational resources, and engaging stakeholders.
samples.ground_truth: To define the scope of the project, ensuring that it aligns with the organization's strategic goals and is feasible to deliver.

samples.question: What is the primary objective of performance measurement in project management?
contexts: To measure project performance against planned objectives, ensuring that the project stays on track and delivers on time and within budget.
Performance measurement is a key concept in project monitoring and control.
samples.ground_truth: To measure project performance against planned objectives

samples.question: What is the primary objective of the final review in the project closeout phase?
contexts: To conduct a final review of the project's performance and outcomes, ensuring that all objectives were met and no significant issues were overlooked.
The final review is part of the project closeout phase, which also includes evaluating project outcomes and lessons learned.
samples.ground_truth: To ensure all objectives were met and no significant issues were overlooked.

samples.question: What is the objective of Section 5.7 in project management?
contexts: To ensure that all project documentation is complete, accurate, and easily accessible.
The section focuses on project documentation, documentation standards, and version control.
samples.ground_truth: To ensure that all project documentation is complete, accurate, and easily accessible.
